title: Gate configuration page + API
analysis: This task builds the gate configuration page and API on top of the existing pipeline_gates table (stage TEXT PK, mode TEXT DEFAULT 'auto', status TEXT DEFAULT 'idle', decided_at TEXT, decided_by TEXT DEFAULT 'system', notes TEXT, timeout_hours REAL). The DB layer already has get_gate(), get_all_gates(), update_gate(**kwargs), and init_default_gates() methods in CatalogDB. The web framework (FastAPI + Jinja2 + HTMX) is established by Task 36 with clear patterns: routes use APIRouter(), _get_db(request) helper, try/finally for DB cleanup, and templates extend base.html. The nav already links to /gates. Stage transitions are derived from the ordered _PIPELINE_STAGES tuple — each gate represents the entry into that stage (e.g., the 'analyze' gate controls ingest→analyze). The task introduces three gate modes (auto/pause/notify) replacing the prior auto/manual distinction, presets for bulk mode assignment, approve/skip actions for waiting gates, and per-gate timeout configuration. All of this maps directly onto existing DB columns with no schema changes needed.

prerequisites.description: Create tests/test_gates.py with imports (pytest, FastAPI, TestClient, create_app, CatalogDB), app/client fixtures (file-backed CatalogDB via tmp_path with init_default_gates() called), and constants mirroring VALID_MODES and GATE_PRESETS from the route module. Create the autopilot/web/templates/gates/ directory.

Create autopilot/web/routes/gates.py skeleton with router = APIRouter(), _get_db(request) helper, VALID_MODES = ('auto', 'pause', 'notify'), _GATE_STATUS_COLORS dict, _STAGE_TRANSITIONS list (adjacent pairs from CatalogDB._PIPELINE_STAGES), and GATE_PRESETS dict with four presets: full_auto, review_creative, review_everything, review_before_render. Register gates_router in routes/__init__.py and include in app.py via app.include_router(gates_router).

steps.description: Write TestGateListAPI::test_api_gates_returns_all_gates: GET /api/gates returns 200 with JSON list of 9 gate dicts, each containing keys 'stage', 'mode', 'status', 'timeout_hours', 'decided_at', 'decided_by'. Verify stages match _PIPELINE_STAGES order and default mode is 'auto'.
Implement GET /api/gates endpoint in gates.py: call db.get_all_gates(), return list of gate dicts as JSON.

Write TestGateDetailAPI::test_api_gate_detail_returns_single_gate: GET /api/gates/analyze returns 200 with gate dict for 'analyze'. Write test_api_gate_detail_unknown_stage_returns_404: GET /api/gates/nonexistent returns 404.
Implement GET /api/gates/{stage} endpoint: validate stage in _PIPELINE_STAGES, call db.get_gate(stage), return dict or raise HTTPException(404).

Write TestGateUpdateAPI: (1) test_update_gate_mode: PUT /api/gates/analyze with JSON {"mode": "pause"} returns 200, response shows mode='pause', re-GET confirms persistence. (2) test_update_gate_invalid_mode: PUT with {"mode": "invalid"} returns 400/422. (3) test_update_gate_timeout: PUT /api/gates/render with {"timeout_hours": 24.0} returns 200, timeout persisted. (4) test_update_gate_clear_timeout: PUT with {"timeout_hours": null} clears timeout. (5) test_update_gate_unknown_stage: PUT /api/gates/nonexistent returns 404.

Implement PUT /api/gates/{stage} endpoint: define Pydantic GateUpdate model with optional mode (validated against VALID_MODES) and optional timeout_hours (float | None). Validate stage, call db.update_gate() with non-None fields, return updated gate dict from db.get_gate(). Use Body(...) or Pydantic model for request parsing.

Write TestGateApproveAPI: (1) test_approve_gate: POST /api/gates/classify/approve returns 200, response has status='approved', decided_by='console', decided_at is non-null ISO timestamp. (2) test_approve_unknown_stage_returns_404: POST /api/gates/nonexistent/approve returns 404.
Implement POST /api/gates/{stage}/approve: validate stage, call db.update_gate(stage, status='approved', decided_by='console', decided_at=datetime.utcnow().isoformat()), return updated gate.
Write TestGateSkipAPI: (1) test_skip_gate: POST /api/gates/classify/skip returns 200, response has status='skipped', decided_by='console', decided_at set. (2) test_skip_unknown_stage_returns_404.
Implement POST /api/gates/{stage}/skip: same pattern as approve but status='skipped'.

Write TestGatePresetsAPI: (1) test_preset_full_auto: PUT /api/gates/preset/full_auto returns 200, response is list of 9 gates all with mode='auto'. (2) test_preset_review_creative: verify classify, narrate, upload gates are 'pause', rest 'auto'. (3) test_preset_review_everything: all 9 gates mode='pause'. (4) test_preset_review_before_render: edl and render gates 'pause', rest 'auto'. (5) test_preset_unknown_returns_404: PUT /api/gates/preset/nonexistent returns 404.

Implement PUT /api/gates/preset/{preset_name}: validate preset_name in GATE_PRESETS, iterate preset config (dict mapping stage→mode), call db.update_gate() for each, return db.get_all_gates() as response. Define GATE_PRESETS: full_auto (all 'auto'), review_creative (classify/narrate/upload → 'pause'), review_everything (all 'pause'), review_before_render (edl/render → 'pause').

Write TestGatesPage: (1) test_gates_page_returns_200: GET /gates returns 200 with text/html. (2) test_gates_page_has_title: HTML contains 'Gate Configuration'. (3) test_gates_page_shows_all_stages: HTML contains all 9 stage names. (4) test_gates_page_shows_transitions: HTML contains transition labels (e.g., 'ingest' paired with 'analyze' in transition rows).

Create gates/config.html template extending base.html with title 'Gate Configuration'. Build _STAGE_TRANSITIONS in route as list of (from_stage, to_stage, gate_stage) tuples. Render vertical list of transitions, each including the gate_toggle partial. Add GET /gates route that queries all gates, builds transitions context, renders template.

Write TestGateTogglePartial: (1) test_toggle_contains_mode_select: gate_toggle partial HTML contains a select/dropdown with options 'auto', 'pause', 'notify'. (2) test_toggle_has_htmx_attributes: HTML contains hx-put and hx-swap attributes for HTMX gate update. (3) test_toggle_shows_current_mode: the selected option matches the gate's current mode.

Create partials/gate_toggle.html: a div with id='gate-{stage}' containing from→to stage labels, mode select with hx-put='/api/gates/{stage}' hx-target='#gate-{stage}' hx-swap='outerHTML', timeout_hours input field, status badge with color class, and conditional approve/skip buttons when status='waiting' with hx-post targeting approve/skip endpoints.

Write TestGatePresetsUI: (1) test_gates_page_shows_preset_buttons: GET /gates HTML contains buttons/links for all 4 presets (Full Auto, Review Creative, Review Everything, Review Before Render). (2) test_preset_buttons_have_htmx: each button has hx-put targeting /api/gates/preset/{name}.

Add presets section to gates/config.html above the gate list: a row of 4 styled buttons, each with hx-put='/api/gates/preset/{name}' hx-target='#gate-list' hx-swap='innerHTML' to replace the entire gate list after preset application.

Write TestGateStatusDisplay: (1) test_waiting_gate_shows_approve_skip_buttons: set a gate to status='waiting', GET /gates HTML shows 'Approve' and 'Skip' buttons for that gate. (2) test_approved_gate_shows_decided_info: set status='approved' with decided_at and decided_by, verify these appear in HTML. (3) test_status_color_coding: waiting gate has amber class, approved has green class, idle has gray class.

Enhance gate_toggle.html: add status badge with conditional Tailwind color classes (idle=gray, waiting=amber, approved=green, rejected=red, skipped=blue), decided_at/decided_by display when set, and prominent approve/skip buttons when status='waiting'. Add gate-specific CSS to app.css for gate row styling and status badge colors.

Write TestHtmxResponses: (1) test_put_gate_returns_html_for_htmx: PUT /api/gates/analyze with HX-Request header returns text/html containing the gate_toggle partial. (2) test_put_gate_returns_json_without_htmx: same PUT without HX-Request returns application/json. (3) test_approve_returns_html_for_htmx: POST approve with HX-Request returns HTML partial. (4) test_skip_returns_html_for_htmx similarly.

Add HTMX-aware response logic: in PUT /api/gates/{stage}, POST approve, POST skip, check request.headers.get('hx-request'). If present, render gate_toggle.html partial with updated gate data and return HTMLResponse. Otherwise return JSON dict. Build _render_gate_partial() helper to avoid duplication.

Write TestPresetHtmxResponse: (1) test_preset_returns_html_for_htmx: PUT /api/gates/preset/full_auto with HX-Request header returns HTML containing all 9 gate toggle rows (one per stage). (2) test_preset_returns_json_without_htmx: same request without HX-Request returns JSON list.

Add HTMX-aware response to preset endpoint: when HX-Request present, render all gate_toggle partials concatenated and return as HTMLResponse for swapping the gate list container. Add refreshGateRow() JS helper to app.js for potential SSE-driven gate updates. Final polish: verify all endpoints work end-to-end, templates render correctly, HTMX interactions are wired.

design_decisions.decision: Gate modes are 'auto', 'pause', 'notify' stored as text in existing mode column
design_decisions.rationale: The DB mode column is TEXT with no constraint. The prior 'manual' value maps conceptually to 'pause'. No schema migration needed — we validate in the route layer via VALID_MODES constant.

design_decisions.decision: Stage transitions derived from ordered _PIPELINE_STAGES tuple as adjacent pairs
design_decisions.rationale: The 9 pipeline stages form a linear sequence. Each gate controls entry into its stage, so 'analyze' gate = transition from ingest→analyze. The first stage (ingest) gate controls pipeline entry. This gives 8 transition pairs plus the ingest entry gate = 9 total gate rows.

design_decisions.decision: Presets defined as Python dicts in gates.py, not in the database
design_decisions.rationale: Presets are fixed business logic (Full Auto, Review Creative, Review Everything, Review Before Render). Storing them in code keeps them version-controlled and avoids an extra DB table. Each preset maps stage names to modes; stages not mentioned default to 'auto'.

design_decisions.decision: HTMX-aware endpoints: check HX-Request header to return HTML partial or JSON
design_decisions.rationale: Follows the existing pattern in media.py where /api/media returns HTML for HTMX requests and JSON otherwise. This enables both the interactive HTMX UI and programmatic JSON API from the same endpoints.

design_decisions.decision: Gate statuses: idle, waiting, approved, rejected, skipped (gate-specific, distinct from job execution statuses)
design_decisions.rationale: The pipeline_gates.status column represents gate decision state, not job execution state. These statuses reflect the gate lifecycle: idle (no decision needed), waiting (paused for human), approved/rejected/skipped (decision made). This is consistent with the task description.

design_decisions.decision: approve/skip actions set decided_by='console' to distinguish from programmatic/CLI decisions
design_decisions.rationale: The decided_by field tracks provenance. Web console actions are always 'console'. Future CLI or API integrations could use different identifiers.

design_decisions.decision: Timeout configuration uses existing timeout_hours REAL column, null means no timeout
design_decisions.rationale: The DB already has timeout_hours. The UI presents it as an optional field — when set, the orchestrator can auto-approve after N hours. Null/empty = no auto-approve timeout.

design_decisions.decision: Single test file tests/test_gates.py covers all gate routes (API + page + partials)
design_decisions.rationale: Follows project convention where test_web_app.py covers the app skeleton. Gate routes are cohesive enough for one file. Tests organized into classes by feature area: TestGateListAPI, TestGateDetailAPI, TestGateUpdateAPI, TestGateApproveAPI, TestGateSkipAPI, TestGatePresetsAPI, TestGatesPage, TestGateTogglePartial, TestGatePresetsUI, TestGateStatusDisplay, TestHtmxResponses, TestPresetHtmxResponse.

design_decisions.decision: Preset 'review_creative' pauses at classify→narrate, narrate→script, render→upload (gates: narrate, script, upload instead of classify, narrate, upload)
design_decisions.rationale: The task says pause at CLASSIFY→NARRATE, NARRATE→SCRIPT, RENDER→UPLOAD. In our model, gates control entry into the target stage: CLASSIFY→NARRATE = gate at 'narrate', NARRATE→SCRIPT = gate at 'script', RENDER→UPLOAD = gate at 'upload'. Similarly review_before_render: EDL→SOURCE = gate at 'source', RENDER→UPLOAD = gate at 'upload'.

reuse.what: CatalogDB gate methods (get_gate, get_all_gates, update_gate, init_default_gates)
reuse.how: All gate CRUD goes through these existing methods. No new DB methods needed — update_gate(**kwargs) already accepts arbitrary field updates including mode, status, timeout_hours, decided_at, decided_by.
Replicate the same helper in gates.py: CatalogDB(request.app.state.db_path) with try/finally db.close() in each endpoint.

reuse.what: base.html template with nav, Tailwind CSS, HTMX
reuse.how: gates/config.html extends base.html using {% extends 'base.html' %} and overrides title/content blocks. Nav already has /gates link (line 68).
Import and use for stage name validation in API endpoints and for building the _STAGE_TRANSITIONS list. Same pattern as dashboard.py line 26.

reuse.what: TestClient + app fixture pattern for web tests
reuse.how: Replicate the app/client fixture pattern: create_app(str(tmp_path / 'catalog.db')), TestClient(app). Use init_default_gates() in fixture setup to seed gate rows.

Follow the same structure for gate_toggle.html: self-contained div with unique id for HTMX targeting, Tailwind dark theme styling, conditional rendering. Use templates.get_template().render() for partial responses.
Define analogous _GATE_STATUS_COLORS dict in gates.py for gate-specific status colors (idle=gray, waiting=amber, approved=green, rejected=red, skipped=blue).

reuse.what: Router registration in routes/__init__.py and app.py
reuse.how: Add 'from autopilot.web.routes.gates import router as gates_router' to __init__.py, add gates_router to __all__, and app.include_router(gates_router) in app.py.
